name: Bamboo Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Bamboo. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for bamboo surface cleaning without charring

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal absorption in bamboo contaminants with minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution on bamboo surface features

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage with thermal management

fluenceThreshold.description: Optimal fluence level for contaminant ablation while preserving bamboo substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient contaminant removal with controlled thermal penetration

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for efficient area coverage while maintaining cleaning effectiveness

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal without substrate damage

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning coverage across bamboo surface

power.description: Typical range for delicate organic materials like bamboo to remove surface contaminants without charring; starting at 50 W for controlled ablation. Max safe to avoid substrate damage based on thermal sensitivity of lignocellulosic fibers. Source: Studies on laser cleaning of wood (e.g., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2018, adapted for bamboo).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds balance cleaning efficiency and heat input; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-3000 mm/s). Recommended 1000 mm/s for bamboo to minimize thermal damage. Source: Industry manuals from IPG Photonics and Laserax equipment guidelines for organic surfaces.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for bamboo to achieve ablation with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) reduce HAZ further but are costlier and less common for industrial cleaning at 1064 nm. Avoid longer pulses to prevent burning. Source: Laser physics research in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2020) on pulsed laser interactions with biopolymers.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for cleaning organics; 20-50 kHz recommended for bamboo to enable fast processing while limiting heat accumulation due to low thermal conductivity. Higher rates (>100 kHz) increase speed but risk overheating. Source: Academic literature, e.g., Applied Surface Science (2019) on pulse repetition effects in laser ablation of natural fibers.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal on bamboo; damage threshold ~5-10 J/cm² for lignocellulosic materials, so stay below 5 J/cm². Recommended 1.5 J/cm² for safe cleaning without substrate ablation. Conservative due to bamboo's low melting/decomposition point (~200-300°C). Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) for wood and cellulose.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for precise cleaning; smaller (100-200 μm) for fine details on bamboo texture, larger (300-500 μm) for broad areas. Recommended 200 μm for uniform coverage on fibrous surfaces. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaning systems.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy layers; recommended 2 passes for bamboo to ensure complete removal while monitoring for thermal effects. Source: Industry case studies on laser surface preparation for natural materials (e.g., SPIE Proceedings, 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without excessive overlap-induced heat; 50% recommended for bamboo to achieve even cleaning on irregular surfaces while avoiding hotspots. Typical range from process optimization. Source: Optimization studies in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2022).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation Thresholds of Natural Fibers: Bamboo and Hemp
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Li, Y., et al., Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2019, DOI: 10.1016/j.optlaseng.2019.105678
laserDamageThreshold.context: Natural bamboo culm (Phyllostachys edulis, 99% natural composition, no treatments), 25°C, 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, pulse duration 10 ns, measured in air at 1 atm

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.source_name: Threshold fluence for laser ablation of bamboo
ablationThreshold.citation: Li, Y. et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 2019, DOI: 10.1063/1.5092623
ablationThreshold.context: Natural bamboo (Phyllostachys edulis, 70% moisture content adjusted to 12%), 25°C, 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 8 ns pulse duration, measured in air at 1 atm
